title: Cycle slogans in Globe tab + fix perpetual loading bug in Stations tab

requirements.summary: Add cycling anti-subscription slogans with fade transitions to the GlobeView tab, matching the existing implementation in NowPlayingPanel and FullscreenOverlay
acceptanceCriteria: Slogans are visible in the Globe tab and rotate through the same list used in NowPlayingPanel/FullscreenOverlay
Transitions use the same fade effect as the existing slogan cycling implementation
Slogan display does not obstruct globe interaction (markers, hover tooltips, OrbitControls)

file_operations.description: Add a slogan cycling section positioned below or around the globe that uses the same slogan list and fade transition logic as NowPlayingPanel and FullscreenOverlay. Extract shared slogan logic into a reusable hook if not already present. Position the slogan text to avoid obstructing the interactive globe elements (markers, tooltips, OrbitControls). Verify the existing slogan implementation in NowPlayingPanel and FullscreenOverlay to maintain consistency in timing and fade effect.

Create a custom hook that encapsulates the slogan cycling logic (slogan list, interval timing, fade state management) extracted from NowPlayingPanel and FullscreenOverlay. This hook should return the current slogan and fade state to ensure consistent behavior across all three components (NowPlayingPanel, FullscreenOverlay, GlobeView). Use the same interval and transition settings as the existing implementations.

Refactor to use the new useSlogans hook instead of inline slogan cycling logic, ensuring the behavior remains identical to the current implementation. Remove duplicated slogan list and cycling code, replacing it with the shared hook.

Refactor to use the new useSlogans hook instead of inline slogan cycling logic, ensuring the behavior remains identical to the current implementation. Remove duplicated slogan list and cycling code, replacing it with the shared hook.

requirements.summary: Fix the perpetual loading state in the Stations tab by ensuring the loading spinner resolves to either a populated station list or a clear error/empty state
acceptanceCriteria: Opening the Stations tab resolves to either a populated station list or a visible error/empty state within a reasonable timeout
No infinite loading spinner is shown when the Radio Browser API has returned data or an error
Retry logic or fallback handling is in place if the initial fetch fails

file_operations.description: Review the stations data fetching logic and conditional rendering in the Stations tab view. Ensure that the loading state is properly tied to the data fetch lifecycle (pending, success, error). Add explicit error and empty state handling so that when the Radio Browser API returns an error or empty result, the UI shows a clear message instead of an infinite spinner. Verify that React Query's isLoading, isError, and data states are correctly checked before rendering the StationList. If the fetch is stuck, add a timeout or fallback mechanism to exit the loading state.

Review the Radio Browser API service methods to ensure they properly handle network errors, timeouts, and empty responses. Add explicit error throwing or rejection for failed requests so that React Query can detect and surface the error state. Ensure that promises resolve or reject in all code paths to prevent hanging requests that could cause perpetual loading.
